user.title: Set up ABB Terra AC
user.description: Enter the IP address and port of your ABB Terra AC charger

data_description.host: Hostname or IPv4 address of the charger on your local network.
data_description.port: TCP port for Modbus (typically 502 for ABB Terra AC).

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure ABB Terra AC
reconfigure.description: Update the IP address and port of your ABB Terra AC charger

data_description.host: Hostname or IPv4 address of the charger on your local network.
data_description.port: TCP port for Modbus (typically 502 for ABB Terra AC).

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the charger. Check the IP address and port.
error.timeout: Connection to the charger timed out. Check your network connection.
error.invalid_response: The charger is not responding correctly. Check if Modbus TCP is enabled.

init.title: ABB Terra AC options
init.description: Runtime settings (identity and connection are changed via reconfigure).

data_description.scan_interval: How often Home Assistant reads charger registers, in seconds (5–300).

stop_charging.name: Stop Charging
stop_charging.description: Ends the charging session. Starting again may require a new start command.

current_limit.name: Charging Current Limit
current_limit.description: Set to 0 A to pause charging without ending the session.

charger_unavailable.message: The charger did not respond to the requested change.

write_failed.message: The charger rejected the requested change.

firmware_limit_restore_failed.title: ABB Terra AC charger returned an invalid {limit_name}
firmware_limit_restore_failed.description: The charger reported a {limit_name} of {current_value} A, which is above the configured maximum of {max_value} A. Home Assistant tried to restore a valid value automatically, but the charger did not accept the change. Verify the charger configuration, set a valid limit on the charger if needed, and reload or reconfigure the integration after the device is stable again.
